name: Predictive Maintenance AI
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure IoT Hub
services.purpose: Sensor telemetry ingestion — vibration, temperature, pressure, RPM from industrial equipment

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for anomaly interpretation, failure root cause analysis, and natural language maintenance reports

services.name: Azure Machine Learning
services.purpose: Custom ML models — failure prediction, remaining useful life estimation, anomaly detection

enterprise.note: Multi-model serving, A/B testing, GPU inference

services.name: Stream Analytics
services.purpose: Real-time telemetry processing — windowed aggregations, threshold alerts, trend detection

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Equipment profiles, maintenance history, prediction results, work order tracking

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Maintenance API — prediction orchestrator, work order generator, dashboard backend

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: IoT device credentials, API keys, ML endpoint secrets

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Prediction accuracy, model drift detection, API latency, alert effectiveness tracking

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for routine status summaries, reserve GPT-4o for complex root cause analysis — saves 65% on AI costs
Downsample high-frequency telemetry (1s → 10s) for trend analysis — reduces Stream Analytics SU requirements by 60%
Use IoT Hub message routing to filter noise at the edge — only forward anomalous readings to Stream Analytics
Deploy lightweight anomaly detection models at the edge (IoT Edge) — reduces cloud ingestion and ML inference costs
Archive historical telemetry to cool/archive Blob Storage after 30 days — keeps Cosmos DB costs predictable
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
